full_text_rationale: Full text describes CORAL using shared persistent memory where agents retrieve, contribute, and distill notes and reusable skills across asynchronous open-ended optimization tasks, including systems/kernel optimization evaluation and memory ablations.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 009; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Ao Qu, Handi Zheng, Zijian Zhou, Yihao Yan, Yihong Tang, S. Y. Ong, Fenglu Hong, Kai-Qing Zhou, Chonghe Jiang, Minwei Kong, Jiacheng Zhu, Xuan Jiang, Sirui Li, Cathy Wu, Bryan Kian Hsiang Low, Jinhua Zhao, P. Liang
titulo: CORAL: Towards Autonomous Multi-Agent Evolution for Open-Ended Discovery
formato_armazenamento: Shared persistent filesystem under .coral/public with attempts as JSON records, notes as Markdown files with YAML front matter, reusable skills as SKILL.md plus scripts, heartbeat configuration, session state, and private evaluator state symlinked into isolated agent worktrees.
framework_agente: CORAL autonomous multi-agent evolution infrastructure with CLI, shared hub, isolated worktrees, and heartbeat prompts
llm_utilizado: Claude Opus 4.6 via Claude Code; MiniMax M2.5 with OpenCode
benchmarks_utilizados: Six mathematical optimization tasks, five systems optimization tasks, Anthropic kernel engineering, Polyominoes packing, VLIW SIMD tree traversal, and related stress-test optimization tasks
metricas_desempenho: Final score, improvement rate, number of evaluations, cycle count, coverage, local-test rate, attempt inspection, knowledge created per attempt, knowledge access rate, read-to-improvement rate
metricas_cl: Knowledge-created rate, knowledge-access rate, and read-to-improvement rate over agent trajectories
baseline_comparado: OpenEvolve, ShinkaEvolve, EvoX, single-agent CORAL, independent best-of-four single-agent runs, no-knowledge ablation
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Memory grows through attempts, notes, and skills written by agents; advanced tasks create 0.55 to 0.68 reusable knowledge entries per attempt versus 0.05 on standard tasks; Kernel Engineering run reports 663 total attempts in artifact examples.
custo_por_tarefa: Typical 3-hour single-agent run on a mathematical optimization task with Claude Opus 4.6 costs approximately USD 30-60; four-agent runs cost approximately 3-4 times a single-agent run.
resultado_principal: Single-agent CORAL achieves the best final score on all 11 mathematical and systems tasks and establishes new SOTA on eight tasks. Four-agent co-evolution further improves stress-test results, including Kernel Engineering from 1350 cycles to 1103 cycles.
desempenho_vs_baseline: CORAL improvement rates are reported as 3-10 times higher than fixed evolutionary-search baselines and typically converge within 5-20 evaluations versus 60-100 for baselines. Knowledge ablation worsens Kernel Engineering from 1350 to 1601 cycles, Polyominoes from 80.2 to 77.3, and Txn Scheduling from 4566 to 4444.
custo_vs_baseline: CORAL uses fewer external evaluation calls than structured baselines within the same wall-clock budget, but LLM API calls dominate cost and four-agent runs multiply API cost by about 3-4 times.
limitacoes: Relies on frontier foundation models, lacks bootstrapped agent heterogeneity, assumes a reasonably specified evaluator, and multi-agent runs increase API cost.
ameacas_validade: Task-suite composition, hidden or stochastic evaluators, trajectory classification by rule-based and LLM-based analysis, model/runtime choice, and convergence-based stress-test budgets may affect validity.
